exercises.title: A: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: It's important to talk openly about your ...... . (FEEL)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from FEEL, so the correct answer is "feelings".

wrongOptions.feel: The root FEEL cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "feelings".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of FEEL that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: FEEL → feelings: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "feelings" is the word-formation answer from the root FEEL in this context.

items.sentence: Although he smiled, he actually felt very ...... . (HAPPY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is negative from HAPPY, so the correct answer is "unhappy".

wrongOptions.happy: The root HAPPY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "unhappy".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of HAPPY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: HAPPY → unhappy: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "unhappy" is the word-formation answer from the root HAPPY in this context.

items.sentence: Teenagers often go through difficult ...... changes. (EMOTION)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from EMOTION, so the correct answer is "emotional".

wrongOptions.emotion: The root EMOTION cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "emotional".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of EMOTION that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: EMOTION → emotional: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "emotional" is the word-formation answer from the root EMOTION in this context.

wrongOptions.sympathy: The root SYMPATHY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "sympathetic".

explanationContent.wordFormation: SYMPATHY → sympathetic: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "sympathetic" is the word-formation answer from the root SYMPATHY in this context.

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "bored" is the word-formation answer from the root BORE in this context.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.title: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Come ......! The film starts in five minutes.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is hurry, so the correct answer is "on".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: Hang ...... a minute while I check my messages.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is wait, so the correct answer is "on".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: I wish those boys would shut ...... during the lesson.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is be quiet, so the correct answer is "up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use past perfect when one past action happened before another.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Wish + past simple/past perfect expresses regret about the present or past.

items.sentence: The rain went ...... for hours and we couldn't go outside.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is continue, so the correct answer is "on".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
I don't have enough money for concert tickets. **afford**
I ...... concert tickets.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is not enough money, so the correct answer is "can't afford".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "can't afford", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
Lisa acted happy, but she was actually upset. **pretended**
Lisa ...... happy, but she was actually upset.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is pretended, so the correct answer is "pretended to be".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "pretended to be", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
The teacher forced us to apologise. **made**
We ...... apologise by the teacher.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is passive, so the correct answer is "were made to".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "were made to", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence:
I chose to speak to Jake about the problem. **decided**
I ...... to Jake about the problem.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is chose, so the correct answer is "decided to speak".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "decided to speak", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
In the end, we succeeded in making Anna smile. **managed**
We finally ...... Anna smile.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is succeeded, so the correct answer is "managed to make".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "managed to make", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
I try not to argue with my parents. **avoid**
I ...... with my parents.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is try not to, so the correct answer is "avoid arguing".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "avoid arguing", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Everyone ...... out laughing when he slipped on the ice.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is suddenly laugh, so the correct answer is "burst".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

items.sentence: She burst into ...... after hearing the terrible news.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is start crying, so the correct answer is "tears".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The audience was ...... with laughter during the comedy show.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is laughing hard, so the correct answer is "crying".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: He couldn't ...... laughing during the interview.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is couldn't stop, so the correct answer is "stop".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "gave" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "My grandfather ...... me a warm smile.".

items.sentence: The little girl started ...... when she lost her toy.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is began, so the correct answer is "crying".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The ending of the film moved everyone to ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is moved to cry, so the correct answer is "tears".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.
